creator: hiroto.kagotani@gmail.com
text:
Hi,

2008/2/13, Oliver Lehmann <oliver@freebsd.org>:
> Unfortunally I'm not able to reproduce this. I've no librsvg2 installed,
> and I've the same pretty icons your screenshot shows (please see the
> attached file)

I confirmed that Xfce installed in a clean FreeBSD box did not
have the problem.  And finally, I found a small difference
between the new machine and my machine.

It is a problem of x11-toolkits/gtk20 installation.

The build process of gtk20 searches for image loader modules
dynamically, and if it finds svg_loader.so module, it registers
the module in etc/gtk-2.0/gdk-pixbuf.loaders file.  After I
remove the module, libgtk-x11 can not load svg icons and displays
the fallback boring icon.  If svg_loader.so module doesn't exist
at installation time, it loads and resizes png icons.

So, the problem is that x11-toolkits/gtk20 should depend
on graphics/librsvg2 at least when it is already installed.

Should I resend a PR with this new subject? Or can this PR be reused?

-- 
Hiroto Kagotani
<hiroto.kagotani@gmail.com>
